title: Share of neonates protected at birth against neonatal tetanus
caption: Between 2000 and 2022, the share of neonates protected at birth against neonatal tetanus through maternal immunization generally increased across Thailand, Mexico, Brazil, Vietnam, India, and South Africa. Thailand and Mexico consistently maintained the highest coverage, starting near 85% in 2000 and reaching approximately 98% by 2022. Brazil and Vietnam followed similar upward trajectories, beginning in the low 80s and climbing to roughly 95% and 92% respectively. India’s line shows a steady rise from around 80% to nearly 90%, while South Africa started lower at about 68%, dipped to 60% in 2005, then recovered to hover near 88% by the end of the period. All six countries show net gains over the two decades, with most lines converging above 85% after 2015. The overall pattern reflects sustained improvement in maternal tetanus immunization coverage across these nations, with South Africa exhibiting the largest absolute gain despite early volatility.
